rulings.Case Title: KRISTEN ANN BARTOK VS. STEVEN WANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 28, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT STEVEN WANG, ACCESS CAPITAL INVESTMENTS FUND TWO, LP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION *NOTICE* is DENIED. Trial is May 27, 2025. Defendant set the hearing on this motion within 30 days of the trial date, in violation of CCP section 437(c)(3). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAIM NAZZAL VS. AMANDA SILK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT AMANDA SILK , INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA SILK & LACE OCCASIONS MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Defendant shall file and serve a response to the complaint within 5 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Defendant admits to receiving the summons and complaint by posting and mailing, consistent with the registered process server's proof of service. Nothing in defendant's cited authority supports the specific interpretation that the word "forthwith" means within less than three days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
